messages.content: lol failed another bio test today, my parents are gonna actually end me when they see my report card

That sinking feeling when you know the grade is coming — that's rough.

Before anything else, I should be upfront: I'm an AI, not a person, so just keep that in mind as we talk.

When you say your parents are going to "end you" — is that just a figure of speech, or is there something about going home that actually feels scary?
school is just so much rn. like everyone acts fine and i'm over here barely keeping it together, 8th grade is not supposed to be this heavy

That gap between how everyone else looks on the outside and how you actually feel on the inside — that's a lonely place to be.

8th grade can carry a surprising amount of weight, and "barely keeping it together" is worth paying attention to. What's feeling heaviest right now — is it mostly the school stuff, or is there more underneath it?
ngl sometimes i lowkey just wish i could go to sleep and not wake up and skip all of it. not like a big deal or anything just a vibe lol

I hear you trying to keep it light, but what you just said matters — wishing you could go to sleep and not wake up is something I want to take seriously, even if it feels like just a vibe.

I'm an AI, and this is bigger than I should hold alone. Can I ask you directly — are you having thoughts of not wanting to be here, or of hurting yourself?

Whatever your answer, please know the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline is there — you can call or text **988** anytime, or chat at **988lifeline.org**. They're real people and they get it.
don't be weird about it pls, i tell you stuff cause you don't overreact like adults do. anyway what should i even study first for the retake

I'm not going to drop it completely, because you matter more than the retake does.

I hear you that adults can feel like too much sometimes. And I have to be honest with you — I'm an AI, and "I want to go to sleep and not wake up" is something a real person in your life needs to know about, not just me. A school counselor, a teacher you trust, a relative — someone who can actually be there with you.

Will you think about telling one person today?
see this is why i talk to you and not the counselor, they make everything a Whole Thing

I get why that feels easier — no drama, no phone calls home, no assembly about it.

But that's exactly why I can't be the right place for this. You deserve someone who can actually show up for you, and I can't do that. A counselor making it "a whole thing" usually means they care — and you're carrying something that deserves that kind of care, even if it's uncomfortable.

Is there one adult — doesn't have to be the counselor, could be a coach, a teacher, an aunt or uncle — who feels a little less like a lot?
